tasks.title: TUI: replace manual list highlight overlay with ListState highlight_style
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/render/panels.rs`: `draw_task_list` renders a `List`, then manually draws a full-width blue `Paragraph` to indicate selection.
Manual overlay highlighting is atypical in Ratatui and risks erasing the list row content depending on widget behavior.
plan: Refactor task list rendering to use `ListState` + `highlight_style`/`highlight_symbol`.
Remove the manual highlight overlay pass.
Add rendering tests that assert selected row text is still visible and highlighted.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: truncate/filter-summary rendering to avoid title overflow
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: `filter_summary` can include multiple `scope=...` tokens and an unbounded `query=...` string.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/render/panels.rs`: task list title includes `filter_summary` with no truncation, so it can overflow/clamp on smaller terminals.
plan: Change filter summary rendering to be width-aware and truncated (with ellipsis) when needed.
Prefer compact tokens (counts) over fully inlining long lists into the title.
Add tests covering long query/tag/scope summaries on small terminal widths.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: apply search/tag/scope filters live while typing (preview + cancel restore)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/events/search.rs`: filters are only applied on Enter; typing does not update the list.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/events/filter.rs`: tag/scope filters are only applied on Enter; typing does not update the list.
plan: Store the prior filter state when entering search/tag/scope input modes.
Apply filter changes on each keystroke for a live preview of results.
On Esc, restore the prior filter state; on Enter, commit.
Add tests covering live preview and cancel restore.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: implement mouse scrolling/click selection
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: terminal setup enables `EnableMouseCapture`.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: input loop only handles `Event::Key` and ignores non-key events (no `Event::Mouse` handling).
plan: Decide whether the TUI should support mouse interactions; prefer the simplest correct path.
Handle mouse wheel/click events in the input loop.
Map wheel to scrolling for the focused panel and click to selection.
Add tests where feasible and include manual QA steps for mouse behavior.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: standardize confirm dialog keybindings (j/k, arrows, sensible defaults)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/events/confirm.rs`: `ConfirmRevert` navigation uses Up/Down only (no j/k), unlike other TUI navigation modes.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/render/overlays.rs`: revert dialog instructs “Up/Down: select”, reinforcing the inconsistency.
plan: Standardize confirm dialog navigation keys across confirm modes (arrows + vim keys).
Ensure Enter activates the currently selected option and Esc maps to a clear default.
Update overlay/footer hints to match behavior.
Add tests for confirm revert navigation via both arrows and j/k.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: add “jump to task by ID” command (palette + input)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/events/palette.rs`: `PaletteCommand` enum has no “select task by ID” command.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: app tracks an `id_to_index` map and can rebuild it (`ensure_id_index_map`), which can back a jump-to-ID feature.
`docs/tui-task-management.md`: “Jump-to-ID” is listed as a suggested enhancement but is not implemented.
plan: Add a palette command and an input mode/overlay for entering a task ID (e.g., RQ-####).
Use the id->index cache to select the task and adjust scroll to keep it visible.
Handle missing/invalid IDs with a clear status message.
Add tests for successful jumps and failure modes.
Update `docs/tui-task-management.md` to document the new workflow.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: direct set-status/set-priority commands + optional auto-archive on terminal
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/task_edit.rs`: task status and priority updates are currently “cycle” only (`cycle_status` / `cycle_priority`).
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: archiving terminal tasks is a separate explicit command (`archive_terminal_tasks`).
`docs/tui-task-management.md`: notes the TUI requires a 2-step “set terminal status then archive” workflow and suggests auto-archive / direct set-status as improvements.
plan: Add palette commands for setting an explicit status (Draft/Todo/Doing/Done/Rejected) and priority (Critical/High/Medium/Low).
Implement explicit set operations (not cycling) using the queue edit APIs.
Add an optional prompt (or config toggle) to archive immediately when setting Done/Rejected.
Add tests for explicit set-status/set-priority and the auto-archive/prompt flow.
Update docs and help key reference accordingly.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: improve command palette matching (fuzzy scoring + better ordering)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: `palette_entries` filters commands via case-insensitive substring match only (`title.contains(q)`), with no scoring or ranking.
plan: Introduce a small fuzzy scoring/ranking for palette entries (keeping stable ordering when query is empty).
Sort entries by score and add tie-breakers for predictable results.
Add tests that lock in ranking behavior for common queries.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: add a mode/status bar for discoverability (mode, dirty/save, filters)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/render/mod.rs`: base UI renders list+details plus a footer, but there is no dedicated header/status bar for current mode or save state.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: app tracks status like `dirty`, `save_error`, and `runner_active`, but most of it is not surfaced persistently in the UI.
plan: Add a compact header/status bar that shows current mode, selection, filters, and save/runner state.
Make the header responsive to terminal width (truncate long fields).
Add rendering tests for small and normal terminal widths.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: handle resize and other non-key events explicitly
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: input loop only processes events matching `Event::Key`; `Event::Resize` is ignored.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: the same pattern implies other event types (mouse/paste) are ignored unless added explicitly.
plan: Handle `Event::Resize` to avoid stale cached layout state and to prevent rendering artifacts on terminal resize.
Ensure non-key events are either ignored intentionally (without side-effects) or handled (mouse support task covers mouse).
Add a focused test (or a small harness) to ensure resize events do not panic and update cached dimensions appropriately.
Run `make ci`.
